error_signature: Error [TokenInvalid]: An invalid token was provided.
title: Invalid token error when executing NodeJS script
symptom: The script crashes with an 'invalid token' error when executed via the Execute Command node. No environment variables are being loaded from the .env file.
applies_when: Script relies on environment variables from .env
not_this_if: Environment variables are correctly set in the execution context
root_cause: The Execute Command node does not automatically load environment variables from the project's .env file. As a result, when the script attempts to access these variables, it fails, leading to an invalid token error when trying to log in to Discord.
fix_steps: Modify the script to explicitly load the .env file using dotenv with the correct path.
Use the following code snippet to set the path: dotenv.config({ path: path.join(__dirname, '.env') });
Ensure the script is executed from the correct directory where the .env file is located.
agent_instruction: Update the script to load the .env file explicitly using the correct path.
